name: model-scope
description: Hand it a paper and get a simulator: /model-scope:from-paper reads ONLY the reference material, INTERVIEWS you about the visualization purpose and specific requests, then builds the interactive simulation GUI and serves it at a verified local URL (with an optional shareable tunnel or permanent deploy when the tooling and auth allow) — end to end. Or turn ANY parameterised model into an interactive explorer: move a slider per parameter and watch the simulation update, shown in views the MODEL defines — no fixed graphic or axis. Pedagogical philosophy: don't reproduce a paper's figure — decompose the model into the PERSPECTIVES that fit its class and let the user switch between them and manipulate it live to UNDERSTAND it. A level switch zooms each perspective: a decision/trial model → ⚛ Step (one update split into signal/noise) · ◷ Trial · ∑ Simulation; a sensory/IMAGE model (e.g. early vision) → 🖼 Input image · 🧱 oriented filter channels re-representing it · 🎯 decoded readout; an inference model → its prior→posterior pipeline. Animation idioms: a continuous playhead, a process-mode STAGE stepper, and the perspective lens switch. Build a whole PAPER as a few screens (mechanism → condition comparisons → prediction), separating experimental conditions from model parameters and making the claim↔mechanism mapping visible; heavy screens stream via runChunks with a loading overlay. Schema-driven sliders + a simulate()→views contract + a small plotting helper, no build step. Includes a modelbook of canonical families (Bayesian/efficient-coding observers, causal inference, working memory, decision circuits, neurons, RL, psychophysics) grounded in real open source, a reusable code library, and a GUI QC pipeline (static gate + visual checklist + two-axis review).
